headline: Muslim sectarian candidates continue their rise in Britain. In two council by-elections yesterday in Burnley, independent Muslim candidates unseated their Labour rivals by huge margins. The same area last year elected one of the Gaza Independents to Parliament during the Labour landslide. With Council elections coming up in May this trend of Muslims ditching Labour for their own is set to continue.
We will NOT negotiate with the United States about our missiles." - Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi.
Trump administration weighing plan to seize Venezuelan oil fields," - Newsweek.

Democrats swept major 2025 elections: Zohran Mamdani won NYC mayor, Mikie Sherrill won New Jersey governor, and Abigail Spanberger became Virginia’s first female governor; Democrat Jay Jones also won VA attorney general. California passed a House-map measure favoring Democrats, and Pennsylvania kept its Democratic Supreme Court majority.
President Putin: 'Trump's statement about resuming nuclear tests is a serious issue. If the United States conducts nuclear tests, Russia will do so as well.

The Trump administration is considering three possible strike options for regime change in Venezuela: deploying 'counterterrorism' forces to take control of airfields and Venezuela’s oil fields and infrastructure, sending Special Forces to kill or capture President Nicolás Maduro, or conducting airstrikes against military facilities – New York Times
Sudan’s Army is weighing a U.S.-backed three-month ceasefire aimed at ending its war with the UAE-backed Rapid Support Forces (RSF).
Iranian Foreign Minister: If any negotiations take place between Iran and the United States, they will be strictly limited to the nuclear issue.

The Netanyahu government attacks Mamdani and calls on New York Jews to immigrate - Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir expressed concern over Mmdani's victory as mayor of New York, but claimed not to care about the "interests" of "Israel", as he described it. - Ben Gvir said in a statement published by the Hebrew newspaper "Israel Hayom": "The election of Zohran Mamdani as mayor of New York will be remembered forever as a victory of anti-Semitism over sound logic". - Israeli Diaspora Minister Amihai Shikli also claimed that the city, which was a symbol of global freedom, handed over its keys to a "Hamas supporter", referring to the Palestinian Islamic resistance movement. - He considered this a critical turning point in New York's history, saying "New York's choice (Mamdani) undermines the foundations of the city that provided freedom and opportunities for success to Jewish refugees since the late 19th century, and became a stronghold for the largest Jewish community in the world
Brazilian President Lula da Silva: - The summit between Latin American and Caribbean countries and the European Union is meaningless if it does not address the issue of US attacks.

Poland plans to start building a national anti-drone system within months, without waiting for the European Union's "drone wall" initiative, the country's deputy defense minister, Cezary Tomczyk said.

Armenia has\_no plans to acquire Indian Su- 30MKI fighter jets, as stated by\_Defense Minister Suren Papikyan in a conversation with journalists. \n\nEarlier, Indian media reported that Armenia and India were finalizing\_ negotiations on the purchase of the fighter jets by Armenia. The\_ $2.5-3 billion deal will include the delivery of 8 to 12 Su-30MKI\_ aircraft to Armenia in the first phase, as well as associated\_ training, ground support, and weapons integration
Turkiye to host top officials from across Muslim world to discuss Gaza 'stability force' Ankara has expressed readiness to join the international monitoring force, a move firmly rejected by Israel.

The Israeli military has officially shut down the 'Sky Dew' balloon project, which was a joint Israeli-American designed aerial threat early warning system The ~$200 million balloon was tracked and destroyed by Hezbollah in May 2024.

content:
Defense Minister Israel Katz has appointed Itay Ofir as the new Military Advocate General, following the resignation of Maj. Gen. Yifat Tomer-Yerushalmi last week.

Ofir previously served as the Defence Ministry’s legal adviser from 2017 to 2024.

headline: Former Vice President of the United States, Dick Cheney, has died at the age of 84 years.

Hebrew media outlet 'Haaretz' reveals that a senior IDF officer, a 'veteran drone operator' who participated in the 12-day war against Iran and served in Gaza for over 500 days, committed suicide a few months ago, but that his name is still under military censorship.

U.S. troops from the Joint Special Operations Command are likely to be deployed to Mexico for ground operations against drug cartels, per NBC News A final decision has not been made, and the timeline for the deployment is unclear.

Reuters: China accused the Netherlands of causing “turmoil and chaos” in the global semiconductor supply chain after Dutch authorities seized chipmaker Nexperia, a unit of China’s Wingtech. Beijing urged the Netherlands to stop interfering in company affairs and seek a constructive solution, according to China’s commerce ministry.

🇹🇷 MHP Chairman Devlet Bahçeli called on the Turkish Parliament to send an official delegation to speak with jailed PKK leader Abdullah Öcalan, emphasizing the importance of hearing his demands from himself.\n\nBahçeli also called for the release of jailed HDP chairman Selahattin Demirtaş.
Several explosions were heard in Sterlitamak, Russia's Republic of Bashkortostan.

According to Egyptian sources, Egyptian, Turkish, and Iranian contacts took place to coordinate efforts aimed at supporting the Sudanese army in confronting the RSF. ➡️The Sudanese army was reinforced with advanced weapons from Turkey and Iran, where it was supplied with defensive weapons to limit the impact of drones heavily relied upon by the RSF .

Iraqi Prime Minister Shia al-Sudani has vowed to disarm all non-state actors, including Iranian-backed groups. The disarming will begin once the US begins its withdrawal in September of 2026. The different factions will be absorbed by the Iraqi Armed Forces but they'll retain their own individual commanders.
China has been an unreliable partner in many ways, and the US needs to get out from under the sword of Chinese control of rare earth minerals," - Secretary of Treasury Scott Bessent.

German Chancellor Merz invited Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa to Germany to discuss deporting Syrians with criminal records. Merz said Germany will start implementing deportations and argued the Syrian civil war is over, so there are no more grounds for asylum.

Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Khamenei: 'We won't cooperate with the U.S. unless they abandon all support for Israel, dismantle their military bases in the region, and stop interfering in our affairs'.

UAE President MbZ's Advisor Anwar Gargash, key enabler of RSF’s genocide in Sudan: We all made a mistake when the two generals overthrew the civilian government. That, looking back, was a critical mistake. We should have put our foot down.

headline: Another Israeli has been arrested on charges of spying for Iran. Hebrew media reported that a 23-year-old resident of Tiberias was detained on suspicion of spying for Iran. Ynet reported that this person, an employee of one of the hotels in the Dead Sea area, is accused of passing various information about hotels and tourist areas in the south of the country to Iran.
Journalistic sources: An Israeli assassination operation failed in southern Lebanon, where three Israeli drones missed their target and failed to hit a Lebanese vehicle.
National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir: The Knesset National Security Committee will vote tomorrow on a bill to execute Palestinian prisoners.
Israeli Channel 12: Israel prepares for possible escalation with Hezbollah.

Ammar al-Mousawi, Hezbollah's international relations official: Hezbollah's decision to support the people of Gaza is "the most honourable and noble decision" in the Lebanese resistance movement, and the resistance remains steadfast and unwavering despite the heavy toll.
Israel today: From a senior official: We may attack the suburb of Beirut again, if Hezbollah is not disarmed.

Venezuelan opposition leader and Nobel Peace Prize laureate María Coriño Machado has declared her readiness to assume power in the country and oust Maduro. Following this, all of the Bolivarian Republic's oil infrastructure will be privatized and handed over to the United States.
New York Times: Tests of the new Burevestnik submarine are aimed at forcing the United States to accept Russia’s terms and sit down with Moscow at the negotiating table.
Ukraine received two new Patriot air defense systems from Germany, fulfilling earlier pledges. Dutch-supplied MIM-104 Patriot PAC-2 system from Ukraine’s 208th Air Defense Brigade.

The U.S. is revamping its former Roosevelt Roads Naval Station in Puerto Rico, adding runway upgrades, aircraft facilities, and ammunition storage. Recent F-35 deployments and nearby naval activity point to a growing military presence about 500 miles from Venezuela. Source: Reuters
Hamas have handed over the bodies of three Israeli deceased hostages, leaving eleven within the Gaza Strip.

Turkey's biggest refineries, SOCAR and Tupras, are joining China and India in cutting Russian oil imports after the US and European sanctions on Russian energy giants, Reuters reports. Turkey is turning to Iraqi and Kazakh alternatives.

Maldives aims to create 'smoke-free generation' — BANS smoking for those born after 2007 Selling tobacco, vapes or e-cigs to this age group is now fined at $3200 USD The law also applies to tourists who visit the nation

IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir spoke with Police Commissioner Daniel Levy for updates on the search for Military Advocate General Maj. Gen. Yifat Tomer-Yerushalmi. Israeli Police confirmed she is safe after being reported missing earlier today. @wfwitness
Update :-She was found on the beach in good and healthy conditions, Israeli Police report.
It is likely that Major General Yifat Tomer-Yerushalmi committed suicide," - Israeli Channel 12.

content: The search for the missing Israeli Major General in the HaTsuk beach area, Israel. She was accused less than 48 hours ago of being the one behind the leakage of the footage showing Israeli soldiers torturing Palestinian prisoners.

headline: Iraq's Iranian-backed Popular Mobilization Forces arrests four Syrian-Druze of spying for Israel. The four Druze made their way to Iraq from As Suwaida and were documenting sites in southern Iraq.

Israeli Northern Command and Mossad are preparing for a threat from Iraq. Israel is preparing for a potential attack from Iran-backed militias in Iraq similar to Hamas’s Oct. 7 assault. The attack would involve missiles and drones launched from Iraq, followed by a ground maneuver starting in Iraq, moving through Syria, and potentially reaching the Israel-Jordan border. In recent months, Israeli intelligence has observed Iran investing significant resources in their Iraqi factions," - Walla.

Palestinian groups vow resistance until liberation of occupied territories Tehran, IRNA – Palestinian resistance groups have described the Balfour Declaration that led to the creation of Israel as the beginning of a chain of oppression and crimes against the Palestinian people and their land.
Türkiye’s biggest refineries, SOCAR and Tupras, are cutting Russian oil imports and buying more from Iraq, Kazakhstan, and others after new U.S. and EU sanctions. Source: Reuters
India beat Australia by five wickets in the third T20I at Bellerive Oval in Hobart on Sunday, November 2, 2025, to level the five-match series 1–1.

At least two Ukrainian FP-1 drones struck the 330 Kv electrical substation in the city of Zheleznogorsk, Kursk Oblast. Power outages are reported in the city and surrounding settlements. Coordinates: 52.41382, 35.51125

Hamas's Qassam Brigades: – “We confirm the readiness of our teams to work on extracting the bodies of [Israeli captives] inside the yellow line simultaneously and in all locations as part of closing this file.” –  “We demand the intermediaries and the International Committee of the Red Cross to provide and equip the necessary equipment and teams to work on retrieving all the bodies simultaneously.” – “Yesterday, and in order not to hinder the delivery of bodies, we offered to hand over 3 samples from several unidentified bodies, but [Israel] refused to receive the samples and requested to receive the bodies for examination. We therefore handed the bodies over to preempt [Israel’s] claims.”

Kermanshah exports exceed $1.7 billion in seven months Kermanshah, IRNA – Kermanshah Province, in western Iran, exported goods worth more than $1.78 billion in the first seven months of the current Iranian year, which started on March 21, according to the director general of Kermanshah Customs.

Tehran, Moscow discuss preparations for upcoming SCO summit\n\nMoscow, IRNA —\_Iranian and Russian officials have held consultations in Moscow on the upcoming meeting of the Council of Heads of Government of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO).
Polish fighter jets intercepted a Russian reconnaissance aircraft over the Baltic Sea for the third time in a week after it flew without a flight plan and with its transponder off. - clash report

On November 1, 2025, a 9-year-old Class 4 student named Amayra tragically died after jumping from the fourth floor of Neerja Modi School in Jaipur. CCTV footage showed her climbing a railing, sitting for a moment, and then leaping into the bushes below, suffering a fatal head injury. Upon arrival at the hospital, doctors declared her dead. Her father has filed an FIR against the school, alleging that the administration tried to conceal the incident site, delayed informing him about the seriousness of the situation, and that the principal locked the doors to prevent police entry for nearly 1.5 hours during the investigation.

US Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers have detained British journalist Sami Hamdi, who was touring the US to speak at Council on American-Islamic Relations (CAIR) events, at the San Francisco Airport on October 26. CAIR says his detention is linked to his outspoken criticism of Israel and was due to “urging of pro-Israel” lobby. Hamdi was due to speak at another CAIR event in Florida before being taken into custody.
New Zealand beat England by two wickets in the third and final One-Day International on November 1, 2025, in Wellington. With this victory, the Black Caps completed a 3-0 series sweep.
U.S. troop deployment in Caribbean waters has now reached 16,000 personnel, according to The Washington Post.

Several top officers were arrested overnight in the capital, Bissau. Presidential elections are scheduled for the 23rd of November.

He emphasized that internal instability and poor administrative control often invite external influence and political shifts in the region.

France ‘Ready’ to Supply Rafale Jets to Ukraine France signals readiness to supply Rafale jets to Ukraine, as Kyiv pursues a three-fighter strategy built around the F-16, Gripen E/F, and Rafale to rebuild a fleet of 250 jets.

Talks between Afghan and Pakistani officials wrapped up in Istanbul after several days of mediation by Turkiye and Qatar, with both sides agreeing to maintain dialogue, Afghan government spokesperson Zabihullah Mujahid said today.

Uber to invest $200 million in new tech hub in Istanbul, its fourth outside the US Uber plans to invest $200 million over the next five years to establish a software and technology development center in Istanbul, Turkiye, the country’s Industry and Technology Ministry announced on Friday. According to the ministry, this will be Uber’s fourth center outside the US, joining existing facilities in Brazil, India, and the Netherlands.

Venezuelan President Maduro has asked Russia for urgent military support — including missiles, radar overhauls, and aircraft repairs — as U.S. forces expand operations near his country’s coast. Caracas has also sought aid from China and Iran, asking for drones, detection systems, and GPS jammers to bolster defenses. Source: WaPo
